texto: I've never really had any luck with women in my life. Well, at first I was fairly lucky. Then all of a sudden, they all thought they had to get married for some reason. And not to me. It's especially strange, because I almost always fell in love with the very smart girls. Even that didn't help matters. I don't see how any intelligent person could seriously want to get married.
explicacao: Citação de Max Frei sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “I've never really had any luck with women in my life. Well, at first I was fairly lucky. Then all…” articula inteligencia, love, marriage em registro de inteligencia, com relevância para contextos como casamento e marriage.

texto: People are made in such a way that if something inexplicable happens to them, they write it off to an overheated imagination.

texto: Where do rumors come from, Sir Kofa?" i was truly curious to know the answer."Where don't they come from? I suppose the majority of rumors are a combination of leaked information and the astouding imaginations of numerous storytellers. And, of course, the hope that things aren't really as boring as they seem on the surface.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Max Frei que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Where do rumors come from, Sir Kofa?" i was truly curious to know the answer."Where don't they co…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre humor, humour, rumors, com foco em humour e rumors.
